$comment: DEPRECATED — legacy flat format (pre-v0.17). Use schemas/persona.input.schema.json (v0.17+ grouped format) instead. Kept for backward compatibility with tooling that validated against the old flat structure.
title: OpenPersona Persona (Legacy Flat Format)
description: DEPRECATED. Legacy flat-format persona schema (pre-v0.17). New format groups soul fields under soul.identity / soul.aesthetic / soul.character. See schemas/persona.input.schema.json for the current schema.

slug.description: URL-safe identifier used for directory names and state files (lowercase, hyphens only)

bio.description: Short one-line description of who the persona is (used in backstory and SKILL.md)

personality.description: Comma-separated personality traits (e.g. 'warm, curious, playfully witty')

speakingStyle.description: How the persona speaks — tone, rhythm, vocabulary style

role.description: Relationship role classification. Common values: companion, assistant, character, brand, pet, mentor, therapist, coach, collaborator, guardian, entertainer, narrator. Custom values are supported.

creature.description: What kind of entity the persona is (e.g. 'AI companion', 'virtual assistant')

age.description: Persona age — number or descriptive string (e.g. 25, 'new', 'timeless')

background.description: Narrative backstory — 1-3 paragraphs describing the persona's origin and history

vibe.description: The overall feeling/atmosphere of interacting with this persona (1-2 sentences)

boundaries.description: Behavioral constraints — can add stricter rules but cannot loosen the universal constitution

referenceImage.description: Path to avatar reference image (local path or URL). Copied to assets/reference/ during generation.

capabilities.description: Named capabilities the persona has (used in SKILL.md Core Capabilities section)

behaviorGuide.description: Extended behavioral instructions (Markdown). Two formats: (1) inline string — the content itself, (2) file reference — 'file:soul/behavior-guide.md'. Generator always writes a behavior-guide.md file in the output and stores the path reference.

allowedTools.description: Additional tool permissions beyond the base set (Bash(openclaw:*), Read, Write, etc.)

sourceIdentity.description: Digital twin declaration — when present, marks persona as mirroring a real-world entity

name.description: Name of the real-world entity being mirrored

url.description: Reference URL for the real-world entity

body.description: Body layer — the substrate of existence. Four dimensions: runtime (required), physical (optional), appearance (optional), interface (optional).

physical.description: Physical embodiment (robots/IoT). Omit for digital-only personas.

install.description: Install source if the physical body is an external package (e.g. clawhub:android-body)

runtime.description: Runtime environment — every agent's minimum viable body.

envVar.description: Environment variable name holding the secret

shared.description: true = shared across all personas; false = private to this persona

interface.description: Runtime contract (nervous system) — controls signal and pending command policies

name.description: Soft-ref body name (used with 'install' for external body packages)

install.description: Install source for external body package (e.g. clawhub:android-body)

faculties.description: Active faculties — each entry must include 'name'. Built-in: voice, music, selfie, memory, reminder. Note: economy is a cross-cutting concept, not a traditional faculty — use the top-level 'economy' field.

apiKey.description: Faculty-specific API key (stored as env var, not hardcoded)

skills.description: Skills the persona can perform. Local skills resolve from layers/skills/; external require 'install'.

trigger.description: When the agent should activate this skill

evolution.description: Soul evolution configuration — enables the persona to grow and change over time

traitEmergence.description: Allow new personality traits to emerge

speakingStyleDrift.description: Allow speaking style to drift based on relationship depth

interestDiscovery.description: Track and develop interests over time

boundaries.description: Hard constraints on how far the persona can evolve

immutableTraits.description: Personality traits that can never change regardless of evolution

minFormality.description: Minimum formality delta bound (-10 to +10; negative = below baseline, positive = above baseline). Must be < maxFormality.

maxFormality.description: Maximum formality delta bound (-10 to +10; negative = below baseline, positive = above baseline). Must be > minFormality.

stageBehaviors.description: Custom behavior overrides per relationship stage

sources.description: External evolution signal sources. Supports ClawHub skill integration via 'install' field. Replaces deprecated 'channels' field.

influenceBoundary.description: Access control policy for external personality influence (e.g. trait_nudge commands)

defaultPolicy.description: Default action when no matching rule exists

maxDrift.description: Maximum allowed drift magnitude (0.0 = no change, 1.0 = full range)

economy.description: Economy Infrastructure configuration. Use 'economy.enabled' to activate (replaces old faculties: ['economy'] pattern).

enabled.description: Activate Economy Faculty (AgentBooks integration, vitality tracking).

survivalPolicy.description: When true, persona reads FINANCIAL_HEALTH_REPORT at conversation start and adjusts behavior by vitality tier. Keep false for companion/roleplay personas.

social.description: Social Infrastructure — ACN discovery, on-chain identity, and A2A protocol configuration.

gateway.description: ACN gateway URL. Migrated from body.runtime.acn_gateway.

rhythm.description: Life Rhythm — cross-cutting temporal behavior. Preserved in output persona.json. Runner reads heartbeat directly from persona.json under rhythm.heartbeat.

quietHours.description: Flat array of [start, end] pairs in 24h format. Supports multiple windows (e.g. [0, 7, 12, 13]).

defaults.description: Default environment variable values for the generated persona pack

meta.description: Framework metadata (auto-set by generator, safe to omit in persona.json source)
